enonce.texte: Instructions: Match the vocabulary words with their correct definitions.

questions.reponse: A means of solving a problem or dealing with a difficulty.
To put forward an idea or plan for consideration.
A new plan or process to achieve something or solve a problem.
The effect or influence of one person, thing, or action on another.
To make something smaller in size, amount, or degree.

intitule: Exercise 2: Using Modals of Obligation and Necessity

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the following sentences with the correct modal verb: must, have to, need to.

questions.texte: 6.1 We ______ protect our environment for future generations.
7.2 Citizens ______ separate their waste for recycling.
8.3 The government ______ take immediate action against pollution.
9.4 We ______ reduce our use of plastic bags to protect marine life.
10.5 Everyone ______ contribute to keeping their community clean.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text below about waste management in urban areas and answer the questions.

"Waste management is a major challenge in many Cameroonian cities. Because of rapid urbanization, the amount of waste produced has increased significantly. Consequently, many neighborhoods suffer from poor sanitation, which leads to health problems. To address this issue, the government has launched several initiatives. However, these efforts are not enough. Citizens must also play their part by reducing waste and recycling. We need to raise awareness about the importance of proper waste disposal. If we all work together, we can create a cleaner and healthier environment."

questions.texte: 11.1 Identify the main cause of the waste management problem mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: The main cause is rapid urbanization, which has led to a significant increase in the amount of waste produced.

questions.texte: 12.2 Explain the consequences of poor waste management as described in the text.
questions.reponse: The consequences include poor sanitation and health problems in many neighborhoods.

questions.texte: 13.3 Analyze the solutions proposed in the text. What roles do the government and citizens play?
questions.reponse: The government has launched several initiatives to address the problem. Citizens must also play their part by reducing waste and recycling. The text emphasizes that collective effort is needed for a cleaner environment.

questions.texte: 14.4 Propose two additional solutions that could help improve waste management in your community.
questions.reponse: Accept any relevant answer. Example: 1. Organizing community clean-up days to remove waste from public spaces. 2. Implementing a school-based education program to teach children about recycling and waste reduction.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Imagine you are a member of your school's Environmental Club. You have been asked to propose solutions to the problem of plastic waste in your school. Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) presenting your ideas.

Your paragraph should:

  • Identify the problem of plastic waste in your school.
  • Propose at least two practical solutions.
  • Use modals of obligation (must, have to, need to) and vocabulary from the lesson.

questions.texte: Write your paragraph in the space provided.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

Plastic waste is a serious problem in our school. Many students use single-use plastic bottles and bags, which end up in the trash. To address this issue, we need to install recycling bins in every classroom. Students must also be encouraged to bring reusable water bottles to school. The school administration should organize awareness campaigns to educate everyone about the importance of reducing plastic waste. By taking these actions, we can make our school cleaner and more environmentally friendly.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Rewrite the following sentences using must, have to, or need to.

questions.texte: 16.1 It is necessary for us to reduce our water consumption.
questions.reponse: We must reduce our water consumption. / We have to reduce our water consumption. / We need to reduce our water consumption.

questions.texte: 17.2 It is obligatory for citizens to sort their waste.
questions.reponse: Citizens must sort their waste. / Citizens have to sort their waste.

questions.texte: 18.3 It is important for the government to invest in renewable energy.
questions.reponse: The government must invest in renewable energy. / The government has to invest in renewable energy. / The government needs to invest in renewable energy.

questions.texte: 19.4 It is essential for everyone to protect the environment.
questions.reponse: Everyone must protect the environment. / Everyone has to protect the environment. / Everyone needs to protect the environment.
